binding_constraints: No existing audience or buyer access
No budget for acquisition or experimentation
Refusal of sales calls, services, content creation, and cold outreach
Revenue target is too aggressive for a cold-start founder without distribution

opportunities.title: Productized async AI workflow setup for one niche
opportunities.thesis: If the user relaxes the no-services and no-sales constraints, an async setup offer is the fastest realistic route to first revenue.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can build and implement lightweight automations without capital.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: The user currently refuses the exact delivery and acquisition methods that make this viable.
validation_plan: Only pursue if the user allows one async offer and one acquisition channel.
Pick one niche and define one measurable workflow outcome.
Target one paid pilot before any product build beyond a demo.
kill_criteria: User still refuses both services and async selling after week 1.

opportunities.title: Tiny AI micro-SaaS sold through an existing marketplace
opportunities.thesis: The least-bad software-only path is a tiny utility in a built-in-demand channel, but it is not a credible path to $3000 MRR in 30 days under these constraints.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can ship a narrow utility without much money.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: There is no niche wedge, no buyer insight, and not enough time to iterate into product-market fit.
validation_plan: Identify one marketplace with obvious buyer intent.
Launch only a fake-door or waitlist page first.
kill_criteria: No repeat pain found in public evidence.
No signups from initial listing traffic.

opportunities.title: No-go for now with a 30-day edge-building plan
opportunities.thesis: The best move is to change one binding constraint, build problem clarity and buyer access, and delay product ambition until there is a narrow wedge.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: This is the only path that aligns with the actual starting position instead of assuming hidden leverage.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: If the user refuses to change any constraint, there is still no credible business path.
validation_plan: Choose exactly 3 niches with public pain and existing software spend.
Collect 60 public pain signals total across reviews, forums, and job posts.
Keep only problems that appear at least 8 times.
Review 20 to 30 competitors for pricing and complaint patterns.
Publish 1 fake-door page for the best wedge.
Require at least 25 qualified visits and 3 signups in 7 days without paid ads.
tool_specs: Spreadsheet or database of pain signals
One tiny prototype only after demand signal
kill_criteria: Fewer than 60 public pain signals collected by day 7.
No problem appears at least 8 times across sources.
No evidence of existing willingness to pay.
Landing page gets fewer than 25 qualified visits or fewer than 3 signups.
User refuses to relax at least one binding constraint after validation week.

rejected_paths.title: Generic AI wrapper
rejected_paths.reason: No founder edge, no audience, and no credible distribution wedge.

rejected_paths.title: Pure content-led AI business
rejected_paths.reason: The user refuses content creation and does not have an audience advantage.

rejected_paths.title: Broad software-first micro-SaaS
rejected_paths.reason: Too much discovery, positioning, and iteration work for 2 hours per week with no buyer access.

next_actions: Decide which single constraint to relax: time, async selling, service-first wedge, or revenue target.
Pick 3 niches and collect 60 public pain signals in 7 days.
Reject any idea without repeated pain and existing spend.
Launch one fake-door test before building a real product.
Do not expect $3000 MRR in 30 days under the current constraint bundle.
